headline: Senate rubber-stamped 97% of bills without amendment in 2025
context: Dewan Negara passed 97% of government bills without any amendments in 2025, continuing its pattern as a legislative rubber stamp rather than a revising chamber.

cards.big: 97% of bills passed the Senate untouched — is it a revising chamber or rubber stamp?
cards.sub: Only one bill was amended in the entire 2025 parliamentary session

cards.big: 44 of 70 senators are directly appointed by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong on PM advice
cards.sub: Government controls supermajority through appointment power under Article 45

cards.big: Dewan Negara costs taxpayers RM89 million annually in operational expenses
cards.sub: Cost per amended bill in 2025 works out to RM89 million for one amendment

cards.big: Australia's Senate amended 38% of government bills in the same period
cards.sub: Effective upper houses serve as genuine check on lower house legislation

cards.big: A Senate that never disagrees with the government is a Senate that serves no constitutional purpose
Should senators be elected rather than appointed to make Dewan Negara meaningful?
